name: Abhyanga (Warm Oil Massage)
classical_text_ref: AH Sutrasthana Ch.2; CS Sutrasthana Ch.5 — Bahya Snehana precedes all Pradhana Karma
instructions: Warm the oil. Massage forcefully on limbs, circularly on joints. This is Purvakarma — it prepares Ama for mobilisation before Pradhana Karma.

name: Shirodhara (Oil pouring on forehead)
classical_classification: Murdha Taila — Specialized Bahya Chikitsa (NOT Panchakarma)
classical_text_ref: AH Sutrasthana Ch.22 — Murdha Taila (4 types: Shiroabhyanga, Shirodhara, Shirobasti, Shiropika). Not one of the 5 Panchakarma.
instructions: Continuous pouring of warm medicated liquid over the forehead (Ajna Chakra region). This is a Murdha Taila procedure, not one of the Panchakarma — it does not require Purvakarma preparation.

name: Swedana (Herbal Steam Therapy)
classical_text_ref: CS Sutrasthana Ch.14; AH Sutrasthana Ch.17 — Swedana always follows Snehana as second Purvakarma. 13 types of Swedana described.
instructions: Administered after Abhyanga (Snehana). This is the second Purvakarma — Snehana and Swedana together loosen Ama from Dhatus and move it toward the gut for elimination by Pradhana Karma.

name: Nasya (Nasal Administration)
classical_text_ref: CS Siddhisthana Ch.9; AH Sutrasthana Ch.20 — Nasya is the Panchakarma for Urdhvajatru (above-the-clavicle) disorders. Kapha and Vata in the head are eliminated through nasal route.
benefits: eliminates Kapha and Vata from Urdhvajatru region
instructions: One of the 5 Panchakarma. Tilt head back, instill 2-5 drops of medicated oil in each nostril. Pratimarsha Nasya (2 drops, gentle) is safe for daily home use; Marsha Nasya (full dose) requires clinic supervision.

name: Basti (Herbal Enema)
classical_classification: 3rd of 5 Panchakarma (Pradhana Karma) — Basti (Anuvasana + Niruha/Asthapana types)
classical_text_ref: CS Siddhisthana Ch.1; AH Kalpa Ch.13 — Basti is called 'Ardha Chikitsa' (half of all treatment) because Vata controls all Doshas, and Basti directly addresses Vata via the Pakwashaya (colon).
instructions: One of the 5 Panchakarma, and the most important for Vata. Two types: Anuvasana Basti (oil enema, retained) and Niruha/Asthapana Basti (decoction enema, expelled). Always requires clinical Vaidya supervision. Home adaptation: Matra Basti (50-80ml warm oil, retained overnight) is safe at home.

name: Vamana (Therapeutic Emesis)
classical_text_ref: CS Kalpasthana Ch.1; AH Kalpa Ch.1 — Vamana is the specific Panchakarma for Kapha disorders. Classically prescribed in Vasanta (spring) when accumulated Hemanta Kapha liquefies.
benefits: treats asthma, bronchitis, obesity, skin diseases
instructions: First of the 5 Panchakarma. MUST be preceded by 7 days of internal Snehana (medicated ghee) and Abhyanga + Swedana. Administered only by trained Vaidya in a clinical setting. Contraindicated at home.

name: Virechana (Therapeutic Purgation)
classical_text_ref: CS Kalpasthana Ch.12; AH Kalpa Ch.3 — Virechana is the specific Panchakarma for Pitta disorders. Classically prescribed in Sharad (autumn) when Pitta peaks.
benefits: eliminates excess Pitta from Pittashaya (liver, small intestine)
treats skin diseases, liver disorders, bleeding disorders, acidity, eye conditions
instructions: Second of the 5 Panchakarma. Must be preceded by 5-7 days of internal Snehana with Tikta Ghrita, followed by Abhyanga + Swedana. Purgation is graded: Mridu (mild), Madhyama (moderate), Teekshna (strong) based on Bala and condition.

name: Raktamokshana (Bloodletting)
classical_text_ref: CS Sutrasthana Ch.14; Sushruta Samhita Sharira Ch.8 — Raktamokshana is the 5th Panchakarma, primarily described by Sushruta. Indicated for Rakta Dushti (blood vitiation), Pitta-Rakta disorders, skin diseases.
benefits: treats skin diseases, gout (Vatarakta), abscesses, herpes, inflammatory conditions
instructions: Fifth of the 5 Panchakarma. Methods: Shastra Visravana (surgical incision), Anushastra (non-surgical — leech/horn/gourd). Jalauka (leech therapy) is the safest method and preferred for most conditions. Requires strict clinical supervision. Frequently replaced by Tikta Ghrita Virechana in modern settings due to regulatory restrictions.

name: Udvartana (Herbal Powder Massage)
classical_classification: Lekhana Karma — Specialized Bahya Chikitsa (NOT Panchakarma)
classical_text_ref: AH Sutrasthana Ch.2; CS Sutrasthana Ch.3 — Udvartana is a Dinacharya (daily routine) Lekhana measure, not one of the 5 Panchakarma. Indicated for Sthoulya (obesity) and Kapha excess.
instructions: Massage with dry herbal powders applied against hair direction (Pratiloma). This is Lekhana Karma — a specialized therapy for obesity and Kapha disorders. It is NOT one of the 5 Panchakarma. Can be used as standalone or combined with Panchakarma as adjunct.

name: Kati Basti (Lower Back Oil Pool)
classical_classification: Specialized Bahya Chikitsa — Snehana variant (NOT Panchakarma)
classical_text_ref: Ashtanga Hridayam Chikitsa Sthana — Kati Basti is a regional oil-pooling technique for Vata in the lumbar region. Not one of the 5 Panchakarma but a component therapy.
benefits: treats lower back pain, sciatica, lumbar spondylosis
herbs_oils_used: Black gram (Masha) dough for the bund
instructions: A dough ring (Bund) is created on the lower back using black gram paste and filled with warm medicated oil. The oil is retained for 20-30 minutes to allow deep Snehana. This is a regional Bahya Snehana technique — an adjunct to Panchakarma, not one of the 5 Pradhana Karma.
